_comment: Patterns that must never appear in committed code. Each rule has a teaching message.

rules.message: console.log is not allowed in production code. Use your project's structured logger instead. console.log produces unstructured output that breaks log aggregation. Replace with: logger.info({...}, 'message') or equivalent.
console.debug/warn/error is not allowed in production code. Use your project's structured logger with the appropriate level.
Commented-out code detected. Delete it — git history preserves previous versions. Commented code is noise that confuses agents reading the codebase.
TODO without issue reference. Either fix it inline or add a reference: TODO(#123): description. Untracked TODOs disappear from view and confuse future agents.
FIXME without issue reference. Link to a tracking issue: FIXME(#123): description.
Potential hardcoded secret detected. Secrets must never be committed. Use environment variables via .env (gitignored). See .env.example for the pattern.
TypeScript 'any' type defeats type safety. Use a specific type, unknown, or a generic. If you genuinely need escape-hatch typing, use a type assertion with a comment explaining why.
